headline: Nebius Bond Raise And Mega AI Deals Reframe Valuation And Risk Profile
summary: Nebius Group (NasdaqGS:NBIS) closed an upsized US$4b convertible bond offering, increased from US$3.75b, to fund AI data center expansion. The raise follows a US$27b, five year AI infrastructure agreement with Meta Platforms focused on large scale compute capacity. Nebius deepened its relationship with NVIDIA and launched an Enterprise Readiness Initiative to support AI native solutions for startups and enterprises. The company also announced a global cybersecurity alliance with CrowdStrike,...

Nebius deepened its relationship with NVIDIA and launched an Enterprise Readiness Initiative to support AI native solutions for startups and enterprises. headline: Alibaba, Tencent Shares Lose $66 Billion as AI Vision Falls Flat
summary: Alibaba’s US shares fell their most since October, following Tencent’s worst drubbing in almost a year on Thursday.  Investors that had piled into the sector’s biggest names over the past week — betting the advent of OpenClaw-style AI agents would galvanize the industry — reversed course after disappointing results, with no clear path to monetization in sight.  While those outlays remain a fraction of the $650 billion that US hyperscalers like Meta Platforms Inc. and Amazon.com Inc. are spending this year alone, the rising budgets coincide with a Chinese consumer downturn that’s compressing margins.

headline: Meta Joins OCI Group As AI Links And Valuation Draw Investor Focus
summary: Meta Platforms joined AMD, Broadcom, Microsoft, NVIDIA and OpenAI to form the Optical Compute Interconnect (OCI) MSA, an industry group focused on open optical links for AI infrastructure. The OCI MSA aims to create an open, multi vendor specification for high performance, power efficient optical interconnects used in large scale AI clusters. This move positions Meta as an active participant in setting technical standards that could influence how future AI data centers are built and...

headline: OpenAI’s 2026 Scorecard: A String of Lawsuits, Losses, and Broken Promises

Amid the challenges it faces in 2026, co-founder Elon Musk is heading to trial on fraud charges, and Microsoft (MSFT), OpenAI’s largest shareholder, is reportedly considering legal action over an alleged breach of contract. headline: How The TaskUs (TASK) Story Is Shifting Toward AI Services And Away From Trust And Safety
summary: TaskUs has seen its fair value price target adjusted slightly, from US$13.67 to US$13.00, a change of about 4.9% that keeps the stock in a tight valuation band. Street analysts are pairing this modest reset with a mixed narrative, balancing concerns around Meta exposure and Trust & Safety pressures against growing interest in AI Services and broader client depth. As you read on, you will see how these shifting calls fit together and what to watch to keep up with the evolving TaskUs...
